content.text: Understanding Lumbar Disc Herniation and Your Spine

A lumbar disc herniation is a common condition that occurs when the soft, jelly-like center of a spinal disc pushes through a tear in the tougher exterior. This displacement often irritates nearby nerves, which can lead to significant lower back pain and discomfort that radiates down the legs. Many patients living in Dubai or Tehran may experience this as sciatica, a sharp or burning sensation that significantly impacts daily life. While the diagnosis can feel overwhelming, understanding the mechanics of your spine is the first step toward effective management. Prof. Giv Sharifi emphasizes that every patient has a unique spinal anatomy, meaning that what works for one individual may not be the ideal path for another. By recognizing the symptoms early, you can work with a qualified neurosurgeon to explore your options and regain your quality of life through personalized care.

The spine is a complex structure designed to provide both support and flexibility, and the discs act as essential shock absorbers between your vertebrae. When a herniated disc occurs, the resulting inflammation and pressure on the spinal nerves are what typically cause the most distress. It is important to remember that not every herniated disc requires surgery, as many cases respond well to conservative, non-surgical approaches. Your journey toward recovery begins with a comprehensive evaluation, where diagnostic imaging such as an MRI helps determine the severity of the herniation. Prof. Giv Sharifi utilizes advanced diagnostic tools to ensure that your treatment plan is tailored specifically to your clinical needs and long-term spinal health goals.
Non-Surgical Treatment Options for Herniated Discs

For the majority of patients, the initial approach to treating a herniated disc involves conservative, non-surgical methods. These strategies aim to reduce inflammation, manage pain, and allow the body to heal naturally over time. Physical therapy is often the cornerstone of this process, as it helps strengthen the muscles supporting the spine and improves overall posture. Additionally, your doctor may recommend anti-inflammatory medications or targeted injections to alleviate acute pain. These interventions are designed to help you remain active while your body works to resolve the disc issue. Consistency and patience are vital during this phase, as the healing process can take several weeks or even months to show significant improvement.

Lifestyle modifications also play a crucial role in non-surgical recovery, helping to prevent further aggravation of the affected area. This may include ergonomic adjustments at your workplace in Dubai or Tehran, as well as specific exercises that avoid putting unnecessary strain on the lower back. Prof. Giv Sharifi often advises patients to focus on core stability and weight management to reduce the burden on their spinal discs. If you do not see progress after a dedicated period of conservative care, or if your symptoms worsen, it may be time to discuss more advanced interventions. Regular follow-up appointments allow your neurosurgeon to monitor your progress and adjust your treatment plan as necessary to ensure you are on the right path to recovery.
When to Consider Microdiscectomy in Dubai

Microdiscectomy is a highly effective, minimally invasive surgical procedure used to treat a herniated disc that is causing persistent pain or neurological symptoms. During this surgery, Prof. Giv Sharifi removes only the small portion of the disc that is pressing against the nerve. By focusing on precision, this approach minimizes damage to the surrounding healthy tissues and muscles. Patients who have failed to find relief through physical therapy or medication often find that microdiscectomy provides significant, immediate improvement in their sciatica and back pain. The goal of the procedure is to decompress the nerve, allowing the body to begin its own healing process without the constant irritation of the herniated material.

Choosing a neurosurgeon for microdiscectomy in Dubai requires careful consideration of their experience and use of modern surgical technology. Prof. Giv Sharifi utilizes advanced visualization techniques to perform the procedure with the highest level of accuracy. Because the incision is small, patients often experience less postoperative pain and a faster return to their daily routines compared to traditional open surgeries. While every surgery carries inherent risks, the minimally invasive nature of microdiscectomy has made it a preferred option for many patients seeking a reliable solution for their spinal health. Your surgeon will conduct a thorough assessment to determine if you are a suitable candidate for this procedure based on your specific symptoms and medical history.
Recovery and Rehabilitation After Surgery

Recovery following a microdiscectomy is a structured process that focuses on healing and preventing future spinal issues. Immediately after the procedure, patients are encouraged to begin gentle movement to promote circulation and prevent stiffness. Prof. Giv Sharifi provides a personalized rehabilitation plan that gradually introduces strengthening exercises as your body heals. It is essential to follow these guidelines closely, as avoiding heavy lifting or strenuous activity in the first few weeks is critical for a successful outcome. Most patients notice a significant reduction in leg pain shortly after surgery, although some mild soreness at the incision site is normal and expected as you recover.

Long-term success depends on your commitment to maintaining a healthy spine after the surgical site has fully healed. This involves ongoing physical therapy, proper lifting techniques, and regular check-ups with your neurosurgeon. Prof. Giv Sharifi emphasizes that surgery is just one part of your spinal health journey, and maintaining a strong core is the best way to protect your discs in the future. By staying proactive about your health and following the advice of your medical team, you can return to the activities you enjoy with confidence. Whether you are in Tehran or Dubai, prioritizing your spinal health today will lead to better mobility and comfort for years to come.
The Importance of Expert Neurosurgical Care

Navigating the complexities of spinal health requires the expertise of a highly skilled neurosurgeon who understands both the anatomy of the spine and the latest advancements in surgical technology. Prof. Giv Sharifi brings years of experience to his practice, ensuring that every patient receives compassionate and evidence-based care. From the initial consultation to the final stages of recovery, having a dedicated specialist by your side can make a significant difference in your treatment experience. A neurosurgeon can accurately diagnose the root cause of your pain and distinguish between conditions that require immediate intervention and those that can be managed conservatively. This level of precision is essential for achieving the best possible outcomes for your long-term health.

In cities like Dubai, where access to world-class medical care is available, choosing the right specialist is a vital decision for your well-being. Prof. Giv Sharifi is committed to transparency, ensuring that you fully understand your diagnosis, the risks and benefits of your treatment options, and what to expect during your recovery. By fostering a collaborative relationship, he empowers patients to take an active role in their own health journey. Whether you are exploring non-surgical options or considering a microdiscectomy, you can trust that your care will be handled with the highest standards of medical excellence and personal attention. Your spine is the foundation of your mobility, and protecting it is our primary mission.

content.text: Frequently Asked Questions
Can a herniated disc heal on its own?

Yes, many herniated discs can heal on their own through the body's natural inflammatory response. Over time, the immune system may shrink the herniated portion of the disc, reducing pressure on the nerve. This process often takes several weeks or months, during which time non-surgical treatments like physical therapy and medication can help manage your symptoms. However, if you experience severe neurological deficits, such as muscle weakness or loss of bladder control, you should seek immediate medical attention. Prof. Giv Sharifi will assess your condition to see if your disc is likely to resolve naturally or if intervention is required.
What are the primary symptoms of a lumbar disc herniation?

The most common symptom of a lumbar disc herniation is sciatica, which is a sharp, shooting pain that travels from the lower back down through the buttock and into the leg. You may also experience numbness, tingling, or a pins-and-needles sensation in your foot or leg. In some cases, muscle weakness in the affected leg can occur, making it difficult to stand or walk normally. These symptoms are caused by the herniated disc material pressing against the spinal nerve roots. If you notice these signs, it is important to consult a neurosurgeon for an accurate diagnosis and a personalized treatment plan.
Is microdiscectomy a major surgery?

Microdiscectomy is considered a minimally invasive procedure, not a major open surgery. It is performed through a small incision, using specialized tools and high-powered magnification to remove only the specific part of the disc that is causing nerve compression. Because it avoids large muscle dissections, patients typically experience less postoperative pain and a faster recovery compared to traditional spinal surgeries. While all surgeries involve some level of risk, microdiscectomy is a well-established and safe procedure when performed by an experienced neurosurgeon like Prof. Giv Sharifi. Most patients are able to return home shortly after the procedure.
How long is the recovery time after a microdiscectomy?

Recovery time varies for each patient, but most individuals can return to light daily activities within a few days of their microdiscectomy. A full return to work and more strenuous physical activities usually occurs within four to six weeks, depending on the nature of your job and your overall health. Your surgeon will provide a tailored rehabilitation program to help you regain strength and flexibility safely. It is crucial to follow these instructions and avoid heavy lifting or high-impact exercises until you have been cleared by your medical team. Consistent follow-up care ensures that your recovery stays on track and any concerns are addressed.
How can I prevent future disc herniations?

Preventing future disc herniations involves maintaining a healthy lifestyle that supports your spine's integrity. This includes regular core-strengthening exercises, which provide stability and reduce the load on your spinal discs. Maintaining a healthy weight is also vital, as excess weight places additional stress on the lower back. Additionally, practicing good posture at work and using proper lifting techniques—bending at the knees rather than the waist—can significantly lower your risk of injury. If you have a history of back pain, working with a physical therapist to develop a routine specifically for your spine can be an excellent long-term preventative strategy.
